product.name: ArSuprem Static Pro
product.categorySourceGroup: High Visible Warp Knit Fabrics for Safety Jackets
product.titleDescriptor: 97% Cotton 3% Carbon Antistatic, Vat Dyed Single Jersey Fabric
product.specLine: 220 g/m², 97% Cotton 3% Carbon, Single Jersey, Antistatic, Vat Dyed Knit Fabric
descriptions: 97% cotton 3% Carbon single jersey, antisatic workwear knit fabric with very high chlorinated bleaching and light fastness.
ArSuprem Static Pro fabric are produced from the best quality combed yarn Turkish Cotton.
It is resistant to very high sun level, with fastness level of 6 to 7.
ArSuprem Static Pro fabrics can be bleached with peroxide and hypochloride without whitening in color.
Suitable for antistatic ESD application, certified for EN ISO 1149
Vat dyed ArSuprem Static Pro products can be used in the most hard working conditions and in military area.
97% Cotton 3% Carbon Antistatic, Vat Dyed Single Jersey Fabric
220 g/m², 97% Cotton 3% Carbon, Single Jersey, Antistatic, Vat Dyed Knit Fabric
technicalParameters: Dimensional Change | -5% < Length < +5% ; -5% < Width < +5% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1

evidence: categorySourceGroup: High Visible Warp Knit Fabrics for Safety Jackets
matches.rationale: Product is explicitly categorized under the hi-vis category and source group for safety jackets, and lists hi-vis as a property, despite being a jersey knit rather than warp knit.

evidence: "Vat dyed ArSuprem Static Pro products can be used in the most hard working conditions and in military area."
matches.rationale: Explicit textual statement identifies suitability for military-area use, providing direct though brief evidence.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Fabric is antistatic (EN 1149, EN 61340) and ESD-certified, which are properties often relevant to electrical environments, but no explicit statement ties it to the energy sector or high-voltage work.
Antibacterial/antistatic finishes could theoretically support hospital-adjacent uses, but no medical or healthcare application is explicitly stated.
